content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 22, 1991

Paul Herman
c/o Supervisor Carole Migden
Room 235
City Hall
San Francisco, CA 94102

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File Nos. I-91-097 &
A-91-161

Dear Mr. Herman:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

Your letters concern whether advice was given to you in
November 1990, and whether a candidate may have only one bank
account per election. My records show that I did provide advice
to you in November. In addition, your letter dated March 5, 1991,
accurately reflects the advice provided.

The following is a discussion of advice provided in our
February 6, 1991, telephone conversation.

DISCUSSION

In November of 1990, you called this office concerning the
establishment of a San Francisco "Friends" committee for County
Supervisor Carole Migden. Due to an apparent misunderstanding,
you opened up a second bank account under Ms. Migden's 1990
election committee, intending to use the second bank account as
the "Friends" account. You then filed a Form 502 for this second
bank account. The first account had debts from the 1990 election.

After conversations with officials of the City and County of
San Francisco and the Secretary of State's office, you realized
that an error had been made. On February 6, 1991, I gave you the
following telephone advice:

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Paul Herman
Page Two

     1.  File a new Form 501, indicating that money will be raised
for reelection in 1994.

     2.  Amend the Form 502 filed in connection with Ms. Migden's
1990 election, redesignating the original campaign bank
account for Ms. Migden's 1994 race.

     3.  Amend the statement of organization (Form 410) filed in
connection with Ms. Migden's 1990 election so that the name
of the committee no longer indicates that the committee is
for Ms. Migden's 1990 election, and use that committee for
1994.

     4.  File a new Form 410 to create a new committee (the
"Friends" committee).

     5.  Write a letter to the Secretary of State's office
explaining what has occurred and requesting that they waive
any late fines.

     In this way, the new bank account could be used as a
"Friends" account.  Debts incurred in the 1990 election can be
paid off using funds raised for the 1994 reelection campaign.

     After this advice had been given, you indicated that you
contacted the San Francisco City Attorney's office and were given
advice that the City and County of San Francisco would allow Ms.
Migden to have one bank account for both her campaign committee
and her "Friends" committee, as long as accounting records were
kept separate.  Therefore, at this time, you do not intend to
create a new committee or file a Form 501 for reelection to the
Board of Supervisors in 1994.

     Copies of your letters are enclosed for your convenience.

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabria
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
                                            Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

sections.analysis:
In November of 1990, you called this office concerning the establishment of a San Francisco "Friends" committee for County Supervisor Carole Migden. Due to an apparent misunderstanding, you opened up a second bank account under Ms. Migden's 1990 election committee, intending to use the second bank account as the "Friends" account. You then filed a Form 502 for this second bank account. The first account had debts from the 1990 election.

After conversations with officials of the City and County of San Francisco and the Secretary of State's office, you realized that an error had been made. On February 6, 1991, I gave you the following telephone advice:

---

¹, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

1. File a new Form 501, indicating that money will be raised for reelection in 1994.

2. Amend the Form 502 filed in connection with Ms. Migden’s 1990 election, redesignating the original campaign bank account for Ms. Migden’s 1994 race.

3. Amend the statement of organization (Form 410) filed in connection with Ms. Migden’s 1990 election so that the name of the committee no longer indicates that the committee is for Ms. Migden’s 1990 election, and use that committee for 1994.

4. File a new Form 410 to create a new committee (the "Friends" committee).

5. Write a letter to the Secretary of State’s office explaining what has occurred and requesting that they waive any late fines.

In this way, the new bank account could be used as a "Friends" account. Debts incurred in the 1990 election can be paid off using funds raised for the 1994 reelection campaign.

After this advice had been given, you indicated that you contacted the San Francisco City Attorney’s office and were given advice that the City and County of San Francisco would allow Ms. Migden to have one bank account for both her campaign committee and her "Friends" committee, as long as accounting records were kept separate. Therefore, at this time, you do not intend to create a new committee or file a Form 501 for reelection to the Board of Supervisors in 1994.

Copies of your letters are enclosed for your convenience.
